textPlain: It's missing out on small, yet good and reliable providers.Some examples:Tilaa (NL) - https://www.tilaa.com/en/Offering compute, storage, databases, containers, you name it. And having personal experience with these guys I can tell you they are awesome. Want to host your stuff specifically in a CO2 neutral DC? They can do that too.Leafcloud (NL) - https://leaf.cloud/Green OpenStack based cloud providerStuxhost (NL/DE) - https://stuxhost.comFocused on managed NextCloud hosting, managed webhosting and VPNAnd there are hundreds more, actually.
If I need to chose a datacenter in Europe, does anyone know which country has the most lenient speech laws for user-generated content? It doesn't need to be within the EU, just on the continent.
There is also https://www.hetzner.com/ (IaaS), https://www.leaseweb.com/en/ (IaaS) and https://www.nodion.com/en/ (PaaS).Disclaimer: I am the founder of Nodion.
Also: https://european-alternatives.eu/
Have a hard time take this serious without hetzner
Surprised to not see Ubicloud in there, which provides cloud services on top of various (including European) infra providers.https://www.ubicloud.com/
For a second I thought it was an official page.  Seems it is a personal initiative.
It would be awesome to have this list vetted for the CLOUD Act. IIRC only Scaleway is “safe” from it, given they don’t have any PoP in the USA.
I've been quite happy with scaleway. I wish it had hetzner prices though
Biggest one, Hetzner, missing?
It's missing Evroc, from Sweden.
Basically, EU cloud = No cloud. Just a bunch of half-baked disjoint services that can't scale pretending to be cloud.
[dead]
[dead]
[flagged]

I don't think describing Hetzner as IaaS is very accurate. They do offer that as well, but also dedicated servers, VPS and more.Personally I use Hetzner exclusively for their cheap and unmetered dedicated servers, as I don't like paying per GB used or similar, I want one static cost per month regardless of anything.In fact, when I think about it, most other Hetzner users I've met have similar objectives and I don't think I've met anyone around me in real-life that was using the IaaS part of Hetzner.
Haven’t heard of Nodion before. What is your main selling point? What does it difference from just reselling Leaseweb?
Could you give us free coupons for Nodion? LOL
Also "sovereign US cloud" is a bad joke
They don't have any cloud or abstraction. You basically rent bare metal servers.
Hetzner doesn’t really have abstracted cloud services, they’re basically just bare metal servers or VPS.
Not a European company are they though. Worst of both worlds as they come under US and EU regulation.
They're under the CLOUD Act, i.e. quite toxic.
It is a personal page, see the About page: https://european.cloud/about/I agree with you that this distinction could be made more prominently.
OVH is far bigger than Hetzner.
AFAIK they haven’t launched yet.
... and at a service level that makes Google look like a spa.
